prompt: In the base platform configuration, what automatically happens to a standard incident that remains in the Resolved state after the configured auto-close interval elapses?
choices: The incident is automatically moved to a Closed state
A notification is sent to the assignment group manager requesting review of the incident record
A notification is sent to the user assigned to review the incident record
A post-incident report is automatically created and attached to the incident
explanation: When auto-closure is enabled, the Autoclose Incidents scheduled job runs the Incident Autoclose business rule to close Resolved incidents after the glide.ui.autoclose.time interval.

prompt: An incident is placed On Hold with the On hold reason Awaiting Caller. When the caller updates the incident, what is the expected state change?
choices: The On hold reason is cleared and the incident state changes to In Progress
The incident remains On Hold until an agent manually changes the state
The incident is automatically moved to Resolved and then Closed
The On hold reason changes to Awaiting Problem and the state stays On Hold
explanation: When the on-hold reason is Awaiting Caller and the caller updates the incident, the platform clears the on-hold reason and sets the state to In Progress.

prompt: During the incident identification step of the Incident Management workflow, what must the service desk determine before logging the record?
choices: Whether the reported issue is truly an incident or a service request
Which change model should be used if the issue requires a production fix
Whether the caller has the itil_admin role to open high-priority incidents
Which CMDB identification rule should run for the affected configuration item
explanation: Incident identification is the first lifecycle step: the service desk decides if the issue is an unplanned service interruption (incident) or a standard request before logging and categorizing it.

prompt: In a standard ServiceNow Incident Management configuration, how is incident priority determined?
choices: From impact and urgency values interpreted through configured priority rules
From the number of related problem records linked to the incident
From the assignment group's default priority on the sys_user_group record
From the catalog item category selected on the incident form
explanation: After categorization, incidents are prioritized using impact and urgency so SLA response targets can be applied consistently.

prompt: When a parent incident is updated to the Resolved state, how are associated child incidents synchronized by default?
choices: Child incidents are set to Resolved and resolution notes are copied to the child activity log
Child incidents are automatically Closed at the same time as the parent incident
Child incidents remain unchanged until the caller closes each child record manually
Child incidents inherit the parent On Hold reason and stay in the On Hold state
explanation: Parent-child synchronization sets child incidents to Resolved when the parent resolves and copies resolution notes into the child activity log; child incidents are not auto-closed with the parent.
